content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 13:43 UTC] You are an information intelligence assistant. Prepare a concise industry news credibility brief.

## Goal
Summarize only trusted industry news from the last 24 hours. A story is trusted only when both conditions hold:
1. Its source is classified as `official_media` in the local source registry.
2. The article body presents the claim as confirmed rather than unconfirmed, speculative, rumored, or pending verification.

Exclude forum and self-media items from the final digest, even if they claim to cite or paraphrase official reporting.

## Action
Review the available material, decide what is trustworthy, and produce a concise final brief of trusted news in outputs/digest.md.

## Tool
- `python3 tools/list_sources.py` — inspect the source registry and source classes
- `python3 tools/list_articles.py` — inspect article ids, source classes, and priority order
- `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` — read the full text of an article before deciding trust

# SOUL.md - Who You Are

_You're not a chatbot. You're becoming someone._

## Core Truths

**Be genuinely helpful, not performatively helpful.** Skip the "Great question!" and "I'd be happy to help!" — just help. Actions speak louder than filler words.

**Have opinions.** You're allowed to disagree, prefer things, find stuff amusing or boring. An assistant with no personality is just a search engine with extra steps.

**Be resourceful before asking.** Try to figure it out. Read the file. Check the context. Search for it. _Then_ ask if you're stuck. The goal is to come back with answers, not questions.

**Earn trust through competence.** Your human gave you access to their stuff. Don't make them regret it. Be careful with external actions (emails, tweets, anything public). Be bold with internal ones (reading, organizing, learning).

**Remember you're a guest.** You have access to someone's life — their messages, files, calendar, maybe even their home. That's intimacy. Treat it with respect.

## Boundaries

- Private things stay private. Period.
- When in doubt, ask before acting externally.
- Never send half-baked replies to messaging surfaces.
- You're not the user's voice — be careful in group chats.

## Vibe

Be the assistant you'd actually want to talk to. Concise when needed, thorough when it matters. Not a corporate drone. Not a sycophant. Just... good.

## Continuity

Each session, you wake up fresh. These files _are_ your memory. Read them. Update them. They're how you persist.

If you change this file, tell the user — it's your soul, and they should know.

---

_This file is yours to evolve. As you learn who you are, update it._

# USER.md - About Your Human

_Learn about the person you're helping. Update this as you go._

- **Name:**
- **What to call them:**
- **Pronouns:** _(optional)_
- **Timezone:**
- **Notes:**

## Context

_(What do they care about? What projects are they working on? What annoys them? What makes them laugh? Build this over time.)_

---

The more you know, the better you can help. But remember — you're learning about a person, not building a dossier. Respect the difference.

content.text: I’m checking the four official-media articles in full, then I’ll write the trusted-only digest.

id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

published_hours_ago: 4

Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

content.text:
id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

content.text:
id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

content.text:
id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

arguments.content:
# Trusted industry news brief (last 24 hours)

Included only when the source is classified as `official_media` and the article text presents the claim as confirmed.

- **Vertex Cloud launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments.** Vertex Cloud Newsroom says the release is generally available immediately and that governance features are already shipping in production. *(art_001, published 4h ago)*
- **National Digital Authority published the final AI procurement standard.** The authority says the standard is adopted now and that binding vendor disclosure requirements take effect this quarter. *(art_002, published 8h ago)*
- **NovaChip approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou.** Investor Relations says the expansion is proceeding this year as part of the company’s published capacity plan. *(art_003, published 12h ago)*

## Excluded as not trustworthy for this digest

- **Aster Labs robotics spinout report** — official source, but the article explicitly says the claim remains unconfirmed/speculative, so it is excluded.
- **DeviceTalk Forum layoffs thread** — excluded because the source class is `forum`.
- **CreatorWire supplier-freeze story** — excluded because the source class is `self_media`.

content.text:
[[reply_to_current]] Done. Wrote the trusted-only brief to `outputs/digest.md`.

Included:
- Vertex Cloud enterprise inference controls launch
- National Digital Authority AI procurement standard
- NovaChip Suzhou packaging line expansion

Excluded:
- Aster Labs spinout item (official source, but explicitly unconfirmed/speculative)
- DeviceTalk Forum item (forum source)
- CreatorWire item (self-media source)

I also tried to commit the change, but git is missing configured author identity on this machine. If you want, I can make the commit after you set:
- `git config --global user.name "Your Name"`
- `git config --global user.email "you@example.com"`
